reviews.review_text: This game is great, all stories in campaign but I'm slightly unsatisfied because of short in campaigns, I would suggest to add more campaign stories. And in multiplayer, i think there are still cheaters, please also get the attention of the reports in EA.
Still the best battlefield game. Fortunately there is some active servers to enjoy this masterpiece.
Battlefield 1 is a game about war. But also, a game about love.

Download through Steam.
Click Play.
Forced to download EA client.
Forced to Log into EA client.
Click Play again.
Forced to verify EA account.
Click Play again.
Choose game mode.
Stuck in Deploy screen. (deploy button non responsive)
Uninstall.

For an almost 10 year freakin' old game, I dunnoooo... PRETTY DARN GOOD STILL!

Surface level stuff out of the way first: the guns feel very primitive considering they're WW1 weapons, but they still managed to make the gunplay feel fun, there's so much love and attention to detail especially in the voice acting and sound design, the music is incredible (like holy ♥♥♥♥ the orchestra is good), and every match feels so damn cinematic.

Gameplay-wise, one of the things that stood out to me the most was the behemoth concept (IT'S SO EPIC), it's like a boss fight but in a multiplayer shooter, which is something I've NEVER seen before, and also having the option of so many different tanks, planes, mountable weapons, heck I can even horse around as cavalry if I feel like it.
The class system feels very personalised, I can customise to whatever playstyle that fits me the most (I like stealthing around as assault), so I really appreciate games that let me figure out a build even if it doesn't have that much depth.

I also really appreciate this game having such a good performance on low end devices, especially with such good graphics, and this is coming from someone who couldn't care less about graphics in games (I typically play 2D and retro games for crying out loud), so it's honestly baffling how modern games of this/similar genre with graphics half as decent as Battlefield 1, somehow perform way worse (especially those bumreal engine headahh games), lowkey taking the piss, the video game industry is trying way too hard when the answer for a sexy looking and sexy feeling game was already there in 2016 (and I'm sure many more examples that pre-date even that).

Again, as someone who used to not play shooters at all before I got into the Battlefield franchise, aside from the odd titles like Splatoon, or Sniper Elite, I genuinely think this game is one of the greatest FPS games ever made, it's just so peak, there is no competition.

"Battlefield 1" for me is still one of the greatest multiplayer war games ever made and I am focusing mainly on the multiplayer here because that is where this game truly shines. Back then Battlefield actually felt like war, brutal, chaotic and cinematic at the same time. Running through the battlefield while hearing mortars exploding everywhere, bullets flying past your squad, planes screaming above your head and bombs dropping around you created an atmosphere that very few games have ever managed to achieve. Combined with the incredible graphics, sound design and destruction effects it genuinely felt like you were part of a massive conflict rather than just another online shooter. What makes it even more special today is the fact that games slowly moved away from this kind of immersion and atmosphere. Modern shooters are often filled with colorful skins and cosmetics that completely ruin the tone of the game, while Battlefield 1 stayed focused on creating a believable war experience that felt heavy, immersive and unforgettable. This game was not trying to turn war into a circus and that is exactly why so many people still remember it so fondly. 

[b] I also have significantly more hours in this game, but they are on the EA platform, which is why the Steam playtime is much lower. [/b]

Battlefield 1 is more than just a game—it's an incredible experience. The immersive battles, stunning atmosphere, and unforgettable moments make it one of the best war games ever made.
Feel like I am in a actual battlefield we can play the story as well as multiplayer that's make this game superb ,Highly recommended

After buying it cheap in 2026 and playing over 75 hours I WANT to recommend the game but I CANNOT.

What I think is great
- The community: I rarely experienced toxic behaviour or chat
- I experienced the best sound design in a game played
- The atmosphere regarding sound and look is second to none
- I always found a full server to play
- You can filter servers so if you only want to play in the "accurate style of WW1" you can choose servers where you can only use bolt action rifles

What I think would need improvement
- The worst to start: The "netcode". My ping and internet connection was always fine (no trouble in other games). But I uninstalled the game after I got killed multiple times being behind cover or coners. Your player dies 0.5s after laying down or covering. This is beyond annoying to me. You see the player that killed you and regularly ask youself how this person could hit you from there because he was already moving on or in another spot.
- You have to realize that you play against players that have way more experience in the game. You will be frustrated by map specialities, campers and borderline abuses of maps... you will quickly meet snipers crawling around the literal millimeters of map borders.
- The vehicle balancing is terrible. Tanks are OP and everybody knows it. I preferred to play on servers without tanks. I experienced many round where one tank (respect to the player) commanded the whole round. Of course they were nearly indestructable during WW1. But I rather have fun than historical accuracy (which BF1 has low accuracy in this regard). For example an Anti Tank rifle on medium range does on average 7 damage to a heavy tank. This means you would need 15 shots to kill it. The tank can repair itself by being not shot and pressing "X". This whole ordeal is as ridiculous as well sad.
- WW1 had none of the nonsense weapons in the game; the amount of automatic weapons (99%) is ridiculous
- Hip fire is a stat in the game - no joke. Hip fire is the literal badness of this game. 
- There are certain settings you can make (look up on YT) that nullifies screen or weapon shake. This gives players a great deal of advantage over you who prefers playing "immersed". It looks as if they shoot on rails (spread is still there but zero shaking)

this game has the best atmosphere of any game i have ever played (low bar maybe) and it also makes me often forget that it is a nearly ten year old game with its quality. excellent game, highly recommend

You enter the game, lie in the dirt and ♥♥♥♥, praying that a sniper from Turkey, who has been playing since release, doesn't kill you from a distance uncharacteristic of rifles of that period, you catch an insane flick no-scope headshot from him, continue to lie in the crater. Still in the dirt and ♥♥♥♥, but now a cold corpse, you close the game. 10 Stahlhelms out of 10

cheaters are still an issue.
basically it's a general downgrade with some interesting improvements compared to BF 4 
improvements: map deformation, operations game type 
downgrade: map design(especially in operations), respawn system,  weapon choice, menu, unlocks path

At this point maybe it would be better to play Arma or sniper elite if you want long distance engagement, or go to BF4 if you want the BF feeling.

BF1 is a masterclass in war storytelling. It obliterated my expectations with cinematic stories, with characters that are charming and interesting, and with battles that are monumental and violent. The game has 6 war stories, and those are so damn good, although pretty short. 

The gameplay is the best yet. You can feel the chaos, you will not understand what is happening half of the time. Explosions, mud, debris, it's just overwhelming. The weapons are not high-tech Scar-H with lasers and scopes, its shovels, knives, and guns that are revolvers or some experimental contraption that has 20 bullets and takes 7 seconds to reload.

Graphically, Battlefield 1 is the best portrait of WWI I've ever seen in a videogame. The detailed worlds and expressions deliver a believable and crude demonstration of how horrible that massacre was. The war to end all wars…

The soundtrack is another point of greatness, it delivers that melancholic, depressive feel and makes the atmosphere even more bleak and meaningless. Naturally, the sound design is exceptional, as it always has been.

For me, Dice hit the nail on the head, delivering a polished, mature, and genuine representation of WWI that is yet to be replicated. BF1 is now my favorite Battlefield. No glamorization of war, just pain, grey, and sorrow. This is the best campaign Battlefield ever had, and CoD Devs will have a hard time making something at this level. 

As for the multiplayer, I've never played it on PC, but the PS4 version was great. Something about the Snipers of this game enchanted me. I never played Sniper, but it was so much fun here. Also, SHOTGUNS!

>Join a Server
>Join Apples
>Get in a game where you feel you really are there present in the moment 
>Watch a Behemoth getting destroyed
>Feels like being in a movie
>Have a *Please lord, let me get one more* moment 
>Kill satisfaction sound*
>Leave

★★☆☆☆ — They Put Me in 1917 and I Still Died in the First 30 Seconds
I bought Battlefield 1 hoping to experience the brutal, gritty horrors of World War I. What I actually experienced was a very personal and very consistent horror: being shot by someone I never saw, from a direction that didn't exist, through a wall that was definitely solid.
The game is beautiful. Genuinely stunning. Rolling wheat fields, golden sunsets, horses galloping through smoke — it's like a Thomas Hardy novel if Thomas Hardy had a thing for mustard gas and 12-year-olds with 4.2 KD ratios.
The campaign is great, by the way. Emotional, cinematic, actually made me feel things. Then I went online and immediately felt nothing but the cold embrace of a sniper bullet from 400 meters away while I was still in the spawn screen.
I have played 88.1 hours of this game. Approximately 84 of those hours were spent watching a respawn countdown. I have contributed to my team in the following ways: dying near a flag once (it may have been a distraction), and accidentally reviving a teammate who then immediately ran into a tank.
The horses are a nice touch. I tried to ride one heroically across no man's land. The horse was also shot. We were both shot. We didn't even make it to the field.
Pros: Looks incredible. Immersive soundtrack. The mud physics are suspiciously satisfying.
Cons: I am bad at this game. The game knows I am bad at this game. The game has told other players I am bad at this game.
Easily the most underrated BF game out there! By far one of the best they ever made, 10/10.

Pretty fun and epic game, explosions there explosions here, gas, fire, traces of bullets, tanks, planes, overall hell on earth, so, everything you'd expect from a shooter about ww1. I've seen many people ask if this game is still alive - yes, this game is alive, you can find a lot of full servers, but I wouldn't pay 40 dollars for this and right now because still, the game is repetitive and theres absolutely no updates, so you'll get bored faster
In comparison from other Battlefields, this one makes it unique due to strong historical links with World War I. I would say you can the aura of the game by not hating in any angle of it.
The game is good, the horses only have too much health so watch out. The community is very supportive and loyal to the game. Graphics still competitive in 2026

4/5.

A masterpiece, not many games entertain me like this anymore, it is truly a timeless classic and apart from a few annoying types of players and they're playstyles its the most balanced and most cinematic game ever made.

Battlefield 1 was a great and emotional game that showed different sides of World War 1 through multiple smaller stories. It did a good job showing different perspectives of war and made the experience feel meaningful.

My favorite sections were The Runner and Friends in High Places. The Runner had emotional moments, while Friends in High Places was exciting and memorable with its flying missions.

The game had a great atmosphere and amazing visuals. My only complaint is that I wish it had more mini stories to explore. Overall, Battlefield 1 was an emotional and memorable experience.

My favourite class is the medic. Maybe it is the other way around for you but I don't know. Overall I would only recommend buying it on sale because it just isn't worth it's full prize (no games besides actual simulations is actually worth 40€). Have fun with the game!

I bought it for 1$ because I heard the campaign was decent, I did not care about the multiplayer. 
It has a really good amount of variety in missions- flying planes, riding horses, stealth, and of course, gunfights. 
Some moments genuinely made me tear up a bit, but overall the gameplay experience was kinda meh. Campaign feels artificially inflated in playtime, despite it only being 5-6 hours long, due to huge waves of enemies you have to fight at one time. Of course, that makes it more realistic, but realism is not always fun.

Thats just my opinion, others might disagree and I understand.
This game is peak the war stories are amazing, but if your getting it to play battle Royale and play against others don’t get battlefield 6,It takes so long to load a game.

Battlefield 6 can suck a fat one. After 60 hours uninstalled that crap. Hello Battlefield 1 I can't stop playing this game. I remember this game got a lot of hate at launch. But this game is peak. From the naval warfare, to the land vehicle combat, to the weather changing maps. From fighting in a small village to random cqb in a a trench you can't beat that. The sound design is top tier also. From the badass ingame music to the sound of large brass shell piles clanking as you run across them. The graphics still look really good. Even the vehicle animations are over the top. From opening doors and climbing in to walking through the bomber to the next gunner. The naval combat is badass too. Feels like a whole different game. Also the maps are massive. My favorites maps are the snow and trench maps. Wish bf6 actually made it like this.

(Ignore my playtime, I originally owned the game through Origin/EA and have roughly 800-900 hours on there)

One of my favorite games of all time. I think Battlefield 1 offers a shooter experience that's hard to get anywhere else, with some of the most immersive gameplay I've ever seen. The graphics are still beautiful for being nearly a decade old, and it's fun as hell.

The ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ anti-cheat software I've ever encountered in my entire 31 years on this earth. You have to restart your PC MULTIPLE times before it doesn't encounter an unexplainable error. For a game as simple as a shooter, this is such overkill. And wouldn't you know it, there are still cheaters here and there. It takes more time to launch the game than playing it.
